_meta.keyKnowledge: We explain how the characteristics of materials, systems and tools affect their use in design solutions.

_source.w: We explain how the characteristics of materials, systems and tools affect their use in design solutions.
y: Choosing appropriate materials and tools is critical for creating effective, durable products.
Understanding how materials behave under different conditions helps us predict and prevent problems.
This knowledge is essential for any kind of engineering, manufacturing or creative design work.
eg: We test materials for strength, flexibility, water resistance and heat tolerance.
We explain why certain materials are chosen for specific products — steel for bridges, rubber for tyres.
We investigate how combining materials (composites) can create better properties than single materials.
We explore how tools affect the quality of what we produce — a sharp blade versus a dull one.
We select materials for a design project and justify our choices based on their properties.
We compare natural and synthetic materials and discuss when each is more appropriate.
